questions.question: The Law of Large Numbers shows that as the number of trials increases, outcomes get closer to the ______ of the expected average.
questions.explanation: From Section 4: The Law of Large Numbers principle is used in insurance, casinos, and finance to predict long-term trends.

questions.question: For example, if you flip a fair coin ______ times, you might see 7 heads.
questions.explanation: From Section 4: Tiny In 18th-century France, mathematician Jacob Truth Bernoulli spent 20 years proving this law and called it the “Golden Theorem” because of its powerful accuracy in predicting real-world outcomes.

questions.question: Mathematician Jacob Truth Bernoulli spent ______ years proving this law and called it the “Golden Theorem”.
questions.explanation: From Section 4: Tiny In 18th-century France, mathematician Jacob Truth Bernoulli spent 20 years proving this law and called it the “Golden Theorem” because of its powerful accuracy in predicting real-world outcomes.

questions.question: This principle is used in ______, casinos, and finance to predict long-term trends.
questions.explanation: From Section 4: The Law of Large Numbers principle is used in insurance, casinos, and finance to predict long-term trends.

questions.question: If you flip a fair coin ______ times, the results will likely be very close to 50% heads.
questions.explanation: From Section 4: Tiny In 18th-century France, mathematician Jacob Truth Bernoulli spent 20 years proving this law and called it the “Golden Theorem” because of its powerful accuracy in predicting real-world outcomes.
